title: Hoccleve's Works III: The Regement of Princes and Furnivall's fourteen-item appendix selection
summary: The repository artifact is the pinned General Corpus copy of University of Michigan CME item ADQ4048.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR/TEI-in-Libraries-Level-1 encoding of Frederick J. Furnivall's 1897 EETS Extra Series 72 volume III. Its exact target is composite: fourteen appendix poems on pp. xxiii-lxii and the complete Regement of Princes on pp. 1-197; p. lxiii, other front matter, pp. 199-216, advertisements, and volumes I-II are omitted. Furnivall's edition transcribes and prints the appendix's fourteen discontiguous passages from Egerton MS 615, keeping thirteen embedded Pilgrimage of the Soul lyrics distinct from a separate complaint; the preface credits Henry Littlehales collectively with copying most of this material without identifying exact items. The Regement base was Harley MS 4866, continuously collated with Royal MS 17 D VI; Royal directly supplies the missing five-stanza presentation block and final envoy. Huntington HM 111 is a separate Hoccleve holograph parallel for the incomplete Complaint of the Virgin and the Regement envoy. Complete BSB volume-I and volume-III carriers, the complete HM 111 codex, Wright's 1860 Royal-based edition, pinned Hoccleve Archive transcriptions, and a complete Google Books page-image sequence for target-absent volume II are cached with exact checksums and target-presence mappings.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is the exact electronic/edition composite, not all three volumes named by the U-M header, not a complete Hoccleve canon, and not one medieval work.

Appendix items 1-13 are verse passages embedded in the anonymous Middle English Pilgrimage of the Soul; item 14 is a separate Egerton passage printed last by Furnivall. Guillaume de Deguileville's French-source authorship is never substituted for authorship or translation of the Middle English realizations.

DIMEV's current item-level records assign ten appendix poems to Hoccleve, including the securely supported Complaint of the Virgin. The other nine are retained as catalog attributions pending item-specific source review; the Middle English Pilgrimage translator remains anonymous.

Harley 4866 is the Regement base; Royal 17 D VI is both a continuous collate and the direct source for specified missing passages. These roles are separate relations rather than a blended two-manuscript source label.

HM 111 contains two discontiguous target mappings: the incomplete Complaint of the Virgin at fols. 3r-7v and the Regement envoy at fols. 39v-40r. Complete-codex coverage does not imply that intervening folios contain the target.

The 292 historical U-M TIFF page images named in the XML are modeled separately from the current public U-M text/page-view access layer; no item-specific production record or complete direct-download inventory was found.

Current U-M No Copyright-United States labeling conflicts with the legacy 'where applicable' and distribution-restriction text embedded in the 2006 XML. Both provider assertions are retained as an unresolved terms question.

British Library catalog records currently expose conflicting signals: digitized/full-coverage metadata survives, but target manuscript images are marked unavailable after the cyber-attack. Public catalog access, onsite manuscript access, and paid/requested reproduction are therefore separate records.

Every cached local copy states target_work_presence. Supporting metadata, scan-data, legal evidence, the target-absent 1925 volume II, and the BL rate card are marked absent without a fabricated work_portion.

Public-domain conclusions are jurisdiction- and component-specific. Provider image statements, service terms, modern transcription licenses, and repository output licensing remain distinct from the medieval text and nineteenth-century editions.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for adq4048
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: File size is 531,051 bytes at the reviewed CME commit.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item ADQ4048.0001.001
alternate_labels: Hoccleve's works. Ed. by Frederick J. Furnivall.
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding at TEI in Libraries Level 1. Its broad bibliographic header describes a three-volume 1892-1925 set, while its encoded title page and body are Furnivall's 1897 volume III only.
notes: The current public item supplies HTML text and page-level views but no verified item-specific production log for the OCR or exact source copy.
The XML contains 292 PB-linked 600-dpi TIFF G4 image references and says the OCR was not corrected or edited.

entities.label: Historical U-M 292-image page layer for ADQ4048.0001.001
entities.description: The 292 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images referenced by PB elements in the 2006 XML. This historical source-image layer is distinct from the current U-M web presentation and from the independently cached BSB carrier.
entities.reproduction_format: 292 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images, linked by historical filenames 00000001.tif-00000292.tif
notes: Current page views demonstrate surviving access to individual scan pages, but a complete stable 292-file download or IIIF manifest was not verified.

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall, Hoccleve's Works III (1897)
alternate_labels: The Regement of Princes and fourteen minor poems from Egerton MS 615
entities.description: The exact source edition encoded by U-M: fourteen appendix poems on pp. xxiii-lxii and The Regement of Princes on pp. 1-197, with glossary p. lxiii and back matter outside the target boundary.

bibliographic.citation: Hoccleve's Works. III. The Regement of Princes A.D. 1411-12, from the Harleian MS. 4866, and Fourteen of Hoccleve's Minor Poems from the Egerton MS. 615, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, EETS Extra Series 72 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1897), lxiii + 216 pp.
bibliographic.title: Hoccleve's Works. III. The Regement of Princes A.D. 1411-12, from the Harleian MS. 4866, and Fourteen of Hoccleve's Minor Poems from the Egerton MS. 615
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 72
bibliographic.pages: Target: xxiii-lxii and 1-197; full carrier: lxiii + 216 pp.

notes: Furnivall's title-page phrase 'fourteen of Hoccleve's minor poems' is a historical edition claim, not a modern resolution of all item-level attributions.
A 1997 Boydell and Brewer reprint is documented by ARLIMA but is not the source of the 2006 U-M OCR.

entities.label: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek complete digital facsimile of Hoccleve's Works III
entities.description: A 300-canvas IIIF Presentation object for the complete 1897 volume, shelfmark P.o.angl. 313 s-72.

entities.label: Furnivall's fourteen-item Egerton appendix selection
entities.description: The edition-defined selection of thirteen verse passages embedded in the Middle English Pilgrimage of the Soul plus one separate complaint, reordered and printed on pp. xxiii-lxii.
notes: Item 14 occurs earlier in the manuscript but is printed after items 1-13; this editorial order is not manuscript order.

entities.label: The Pilgrimage of the Soul (anonymous Middle English translation)
entities.description: The Middle English prose-and-verse translation in which appendix items 1-13 occur. The translator remains anonymous even where individual embedded lyrics have Hoccleve attributions.

entities.label: Le Pèlerinage de l'âme
entities.description: Guillaume de Deguileville's French source work. Source authorship does not identify the author or translator of a Middle English realization.

entities.label: The Regement of Princes
entities.description: Thomas Hoccleve's 5,463-line Middle English mirror for Prince Henry, composed in 1410-1411 and transmitted in at least forty-three surviving manuscripts according to the current METS introduction.

entities.label: British Library, Egerton MS 615
entities.description: A parchment codex containing the Middle English Pilgrimage of the Soul at fols. 4r-106r. Furnivall selected fourteen discontiguous verse passages; the catalog's French-work title wording is retained as provider description rather than used to settle the English translation's exact source title.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: second or third quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: Pilgrimage contents at fols. 4r-106r; table of contents at fols. 1r-3v.
Catalog origin: eastern England, perhaps Norfolk or Lincolnshire.
Target loci: item 14 at fols. 8r-13v; items 1-6 at fols. 17v-31v; item 7 at fols. 63r-67v; items 8-13 at fols. 95v-102v.
The current BL record says images are unavailable while also retaining full/selected digital-coverage metadata; no public complete image object was treated as currently obtainable.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 4866
entities.description: Furnivall's base manuscript for The Regement of Princes, selected for its Chaucer portrait, final-e practice, and older readings. Its first leaf is a later replacement; the medieval manuscript lacks the presentation leaf and final leaf.

notes: Produced in London or Westminster.
The first page is an early-modern replacement; the last five lines are reported in the same later hand by the current catalog.
Furnivall's base-text use must not be generalized to the Royal-supplied presentation block or envoy.

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 17 D VI
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century parchment manuscript containing The Regement of Princes at fols. 4r-101v. Wright based his 1860 edition on it; Furnivall collated it throughout and used it directly for missing Harley passages.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: The Regement occupies fols. 4r-101v.

entities.label: Huntington Library, MS HM 111
entities.description: A 49-leaf parchment miscellany written in anglicana by Thomas Hoccleve. It contains an incomplete holograph Complaint of the Virgin at fols. 3r-7v and the Regement envoy at fols. 39v-40r among nineteen poems.

notes: Written in anglicana by Hoccleve; nineteen poems; Middle English with Middle French rubrics.
The Complaint copy begins at Furnivall's stanza 7; it cannot supply the first six stanzas of the complete Egerton text.

entities.label: Huntington complete 107-canvas digital facsimile of HM 111
entities.description: A complete IIIF Presentation object for the physical codex, including covers and non-target texts. Target passages are discontiguous.

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall, Hoccleve's Works I (1892)
entities.description: EETS Extra Series 61, containing the minor poems in HM 111 and Durham Cosin V.iii.9. It prints the incomplete HM 111 Complaint of the Virgin on pp. 1-8 and the Regement envoy on p. 61; it is a supporting parallel, not the principal 1897 source edition.

bibliographic.citation: Hoccleve's Works. I. The Minor Poems in the Phillipps MS. 8151 (Cheltenham) and the Durham MS. III.9, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, EETS Extra Series 61 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1892), lxx + 270 pp.
bibliographic.title: Hoccleve's Works. I. The Minor Poems in the Phillipps MS. 8151 (Cheltenham) and the Durham MS. III.9
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 61

entities.label: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek complete digital facsimile of Hoccleve's Works I
entities.description: A 358-canvas IIIF Presentation object for the complete 1892 volume, shelfmark P.o.angl. 313 s-61.

entities.label: Hoccleve Archive transcription of HM 111 item 1, Complaint of the Virgin
entities.description: A pinned TEI transcription of the incomplete HM 111 holograph passage at fols. 3r-7v, retrieved from the Hoccleve Archive git repository at commit 5a6024feccb0990790103ab1fc96286c5ec97256.
notes: The archive header labels the item The Compleynte of the Virgin before the Cross and records fols. 3r-7v.

entities.label: Hoccleve Archive transcription of the Regement envoy in HM 111
entities.description: A pinned TEI transcription of the HM 111 balade put at the end of the Regement, fols. 39v-40r, retrieved at Hoccleve Archive commit 5a6024feccb0990790103ab1fc96286c5ec97256.
notes: The archive catalog identifies this as manuscript item 15 and IMEV 2229.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, De Regimine Principum (1860)
entities.description: The Roxburghe Club edition based on Royal MS 17 D VI. It is a complete earlier Regement edition and a supporting control for Furnivall's use of Royal, not an upstream source of the U-M OCR.

bibliographic.citation: De Regimine Principum: A Poem, by Thomas Occleve, ed. Thomas Wright (London: Roxburghe Club, 1860).
bibliographic.title: De Regimine Principum: A Poem

entities.label: Internet Archive / Oxford Google scan of Wright's 1860 edition
entities.description: A complete page-scan PDF with OCR, metadata, and scan-data derivatives, contributed from Oxford University through Google Books.
entities.reproduction_format: Image-container PDF with OCR and scan-data derivatives

entities.label: Charles R. Blyth, The Regiment of Princes (1999; current METS web edition)
entities.description: A modern edition based on comprehensive study and collation of the surviving Regement manuscripts. It is current scholarship and a comparison edition, not an ancestor of Furnivall's 1897 text or U-M's 2006 OCR.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Hoccleve, The Regiment of Princes, ed. Charles R. Blyth (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1999).
bibliographic.title: The Regiment of Princes

entities.label: DIMEV records for the fourteen adq4048 appendix items
entities.description: Current item-level DIMEV descriptions and attribution fields for records 2164, 2860, 2065, 443, 407, 2064, 3890, 2063, 2061, 2060, 2067, 2062, 2066, and 878.
notes: Records naming Guillaume are used as French-source links, not evidence that Guillaume wrote or translated the Middle English text.

entities.label: ARLIMA description of The Regement of Princes
entities.description: A current scholarly bibliography and work description recording Hoccleve, date, dedication, form, manuscripts, Furnivall's edition, the 1997 reprint, and modern scholarship.

entities.label: Israel Gollancz, Hoccleve's Works II (1925)
entities.description: EETS Extra Series 73, the minor poems in Ashburnham MS Additional 133. It belongs to the three-volume bibliographic carrier named by U-M but contains none of the target appendix selection or Regement.

bibliographic.citation: Hoccleve's Works. II. The Minor Poems in the Ashburnham MS. Addit. 133, ed. Israel Gollancz, EETS Extra Series 73 (London: Oxford University Press for EETS, 1925), vii + 40 pp.
bibliographic.title: Hoccleve's Works. II. The Minor Poems in the Ashburnham MS. Addit. 133
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 73
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Google Books complete page-image sequence for Hoccleve's Works II
entities.description: A 72-page full-view Google Books sequence cached from the provider's enumerated page model. It is complete for that provider sequence and verified to contain no target text.
entities.reproduction_format: 72 provider-enumerated page images normalized losslessly to PNG

entities.label: Hoccleve's Works: The Minor Poems, revised reprint (1970)
entities.description: A revised combined reprint of EETS Extra Series 61 and 73, with revisions associated with Jerome Mitchell and A. I. Doyle. It does not contain the 1897 Regement/appendix target and is not an ancestor of the 2006 OCR.

bibliographic.citation: Hoccleve's Works: The Minor Poems, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall and Israel Gollancz, revised by Jerome Mitchell and A. I. Doyle, EETS Extra Series 61 and 73 (London: Oxford University Press, 1970), lxxii + 314 pp.
bibliographic.title: Hoccleve's Works: The Minor Poems
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 61 and 73

entities.description: The exact April 2026 official circular used as current United States duration evidence. Its PDF metadata creation date is 2026-04-08.

rights.component: The medieval Regement as an abstract textual work
rights.basis: The work was composed and transmitted in the fifteenth century; no modern authorship is involved in the medieval text itself.

rights.component: The fourteen medieval appendix texts as abstract textual works, distinct from Furnivall's modern-era selection and editing
rights.basis: The selected texts are transmitted in a fifteenth-century manuscript; no modern authorship is involved in their underlying medieval wording.
notes: The entity is the modeled catalog selection because not every individual appendix work has a separate lineage entity; this assertion excludes Furnivall's 1897 selection, order, notes, and typography, which are covered separately.

rights.component: The current U-M digital item in the United States
rights.basis: The current U-M item is labeled No Copyright-United States.
notes: The territorial rights statement is not treated as a license.
The legacy XML contains separate restriction language, recorded below as an unresolved provider-term layer.

rights.component: Availability and redistribution language embedded in the 2006 XML
rights.basis: The XML permits personal, research, teaching, and linking uses under stated conditions while retaining where-applicable copyright and distribution restrictions.
notes: This records historical provider language, not proof that U-M owns the medieval text or the 1897 edition.

rights.component: The 1897 edition, including its editorial text and published typography

editorial_practices.source_feature: Fourteen discontiguous verse passages in Egerton MS 615
editorial_practices.representation: A separately headed appendix on printed pp. xxiii-lxii
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall selected thirteen lyrics embedded in the Middle English Pilgrimage of the Soul and one separate complaint, then printed the separate complaint last although it occurs earlier in the manuscript.
notes: This is an edition-defined selection boundary, not a claim that the fourteen items form one medieval work or share one author.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley MS 4866 base text and Royal MS 17 D VI comparison
editorial_practices.representation: Harley readings form the base, with Royal variants reported throughout
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall chose Harley 4866 as his Regement base and continuously collated Royal 17 D VI rather than constructing an undifferentiated two-manuscript source.
editorial_practices.scope: The Regement of Princes, printed pp. 1-197

editorial_practices.source_feature: Passages absent from the surviving medieval Harley base
editorial_practices.representation: Royal directly supplies the five-stanza presentation block and final envoy; a later Harley replacement supplies the opening lines represented there
editorial_practices.description: Directly supplied readings are kept distinct from ordinary Royal collation: Royal provides Regement lines 2017-2051 and 5440-5463 where Harley lacks the corresponding medieval leaves.
editorial_practices.scope: Regement opening/presentation lacunae and final envoy
notes: The precise status of the first 56 printed lines relative to the early-modern Harley replacement remains separately noted as an open source-layer question.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Furnivall volume III, including front matter, appendix, Regement, glossary/index, and advertisements
editorial_practices.representation: Fourteen appendix items on pp. xxiii-lxii and the complete Regement on pp. 1-197
editorial_practices.description: The surviving XML's intellectual text target omits p. lxiii, other front matter, pp. 199-216, advertisements, and volumes I-II even though its bibliographic header describes the three-volume series and PB links document a broader scan sequence.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire U-M encoding and exact General Corpus target boundary

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed headings and rubrics in Furnivall's edition
editorial_practices.representation: HEAD elements and nested DIV structure retained by OCR/structural encoding
editorial_practices.description: The XML uses printed headings and rubric-like divisions to distinguish fourteen appendix items and the Regement's prologue, address, fifteen advice sections, and envoy; those encoded headings are evidence of the edition's structure, not universally authoritative medieval titles.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME XML, adq4048.xml
evidence.locator: entire file; especially teiHeader, DIV1/DIV2/DIV3 hierarchy, PB sequence, and SEQ attributes
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the electronic identifier, 2006 encoding statement, uncorrected OCR method, historical 292-image PB sequence, title-page transcription, exact selected text, and embedded U-M availability language.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME pinned repository state for adq4048.xml
evidence.locator: commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c; blob 03f9359334a79094ddf89517dd083aeab85155fc
evidence.summary: Pins the source repository commit and exact git blob used by the General Corpus artifact.

evidence.citation: Target-scoped structural audit of pinned adq4048.xml
evidence.locator: 5 DIV1, 19 DIV2, 17 DIV3, 996 HEAD, 6,719 L, 960 LG, 246 MILESTONE, 1 NOTE, 25 P, and 292 PB elements
evidence.summary: Direct element and sequence audit establishes the exact two-part target: fourteen appendix items on printed pp. xxiii-lxii and the Regement on pp. 1-197. Intellectual verse totals are 1,253 lines/179 stanzas for the appendix and 5,463 lines/780 stanzas for the Regement after excluding a nested three-line editorial note.
notes: Front matter, the p. lxiii glossary, pp. 199-216, and advertisements are outside the encoded intellectual target even where historical PB links survive.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Hoccleve's works, ADQ4048.0001.001
evidence.locator: About this Item; Rights/Permissions; The Regement of Princes
evidence.summary: Current public item confirms the stable identifier, broad bibliographic description, public HTML text/page views, U-M public-domain statement, DPLA NoC-US label, and simultaneously displayed legacy restriction wording.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Hoccleve's Works III (EETS ES 72, 1897), complete BSB scan
evidence.locator: title page; preface pp. iii-xxii; appendix pp. xxiii-lxii; Regement pp. 1-197; back matter pp. 199-216
evidence.summary: Direct page inspection establishes the exact 1897 volume, the fourteen-item appendix order and loci, Littlehales's collective copying credit, the selected Regement text, editorial headings, and excluded carrier material.

evidence.citation: Furnivall, Hoccleve's Works III, preface and Regement apparatus
evidence.locator: preface pp. xvii-xxii; opening apparatus; presentation lines 2017-2051; envoy lines 5440-5463
evidence.summary: Furnivall explains his choice of Harley 4866 as base and Royal 17 D VI as collate. Direct apparatus inspection distinguishes routine Royal variants from Royal-supplied passages absent with lost Harley leaves and from the early-modern replacement at Harley's beginning.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF manifest for bsb11817582, Hoccleve's Works III
evidence.locator: manifest metadata, 300-canvas sequence, attribution, and rights field
evidence.summary: Official manifest identifies the complete carrier, shelfmark P.o.angl. 313 s-72, persistent identifiers, 300 canvases, and NoC-NC rights statement.

evidence.citation: Integrity and target-boundary audit of complete BSB volume-III IIIF bundle
evidence.locator: 300 delivered images plus provider manifest and inventory; appendix canvases 37-76; Regement canvases 79-275
evidence.summary: ZIP integrity, complete image count, inventory hashes, representative boundaries, and all target transitions were verified. The complete source carrier is cached, while target locators remain two selected portions.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Egerton MS 615
evidence.locator: identification, summary, contents, physical description, origin/date, language, and digital-surrogate status
evidence.summary: Current catalog identifies the parchment Pilgrimage codex, 106 folios plus flyleaves, contents at fols. 4r-106r, table at fols. 1r-3v, eastern English origin, second/third-quarter fifteenth-century date, and current image unavailability despite retained digital-coverage metadata.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 4866
evidence.locator: identification, contents, physical description, date/origin, first-leaf replacement, and digital-surrogate status
evidence.summary: Current catalog identifies the parchment Regement witness, circa 1411-circa 1420, London or Westminster, 1 starred leaf plus 95 folios, 265 x 185 mm, with the first page replaced in an early-modern hand and current public images unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 17 D VI
evidence.locator: identification, Regement fols. 4r-101v, physical description, date, and digital-surrogate status
evidence.summary: Current catalog identifies the second-quarter fifteenth-century parchment witness, 150 folios plus 8 flyleaves, with the Regement at fols. 4r-101v and current public images unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services
evidence.locator: service description and quotation process
evidence.summary: Official service page establishes that new reproductions can be requested subject to collection condition, feasibility, rights, and quotation; it does not promise availability for any target manuscript.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services price list 2025-26
evidence.locator: entire current price list; created 2026-03-11
evidence.summary: Official fee schedule is supporting cost evidence only. It does not establish that Egerton 615, Harley 4866, or Royal 17 D VI can be imaged, nor does it substitute for an item-specific quotation.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog licensing notice
evidence.locator: catalog page licensing/footer notice
evidence.summary: The catalog presentation exposes a Creative Commons Attribution notice for catalog content; the notice is not treated as a license for manuscript images or requested reproductions.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, mssHM 111 catalog record and IIIF manifest
evidence.locator: catalog metadata and complete 107-canvas IIIF sequence
evidence.summary: Official record identifies HM 111, former Phillipps 8151, as a 49-leaf parchment miscellany in Hoccleve's anglicana hand, dated between 1400 and 1425 in the current catalog, with nineteen poems and a complete 107-canvas digital object.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the Huntington HM 111 complete IIIF bundle
evidence.locator: fols. 3r-7v, IIIF positions 13-22; fols. 39v-40r, positions 86-87
evidence.summary: Direct image inspection verifies two discontiguous target mappings: the incomplete Complaint of the Virgin and the Regement envoy. Intervening codex images do not contain the target merely because the full codex is cached.

evidence.citation: Integrity and coverage audit of complete Huntington HM 111 IIIF bundle
evidence.locator: 107 delivered images plus provider manifest and inventory
evidence.summary: ZIP integrity, complete canvas count, inventory hashes, and target canvas boundaries were verified for the full-codex bundle.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Rights and Permissions
evidence.locator: digital-object reuse and rights guidance
evidence.summary: Provider guidance says permission is not required for public-domain material, asks researchers to identify and cite specific item information, and assigns any necessary third-party rights clearance to the researcher; it does not supply an item-specific open license for the HM 111 IIIF image files.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Hoccleve's Works I (EETS ES 61, 1892), complete BSB scan
evidence.locator: Complaint of the Virgin pp. 1-8; Regement envoy p. 61
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies the two HM 111 target parallels printed in volume I and confirms that this carrier is a supporting parallel, not the source of the complete adq4048 target.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF manifest for bsb11665059, Hoccleve's Works I
evidence.locator: manifest metadata, 358-canvas sequence, attribution, and rights field
evidence.summary: Official manifest identifies the complete 1892 carrier, shelfmark P.o.angl. 313 s-61, persistent identifiers, 358 canvases, and NoC-NC rights statement.

evidence.citation: Integrity and target-boundary audit of complete BSB volume-I IIIF bundle
evidence.locator: 358 delivered images plus provider manifest and inventory; target parallels at canvases 79-86 and 139
evidence.summary: ZIP integrity, complete image count, inventory hashes, and both target-parallel mappings were verified.

evidence.citation: Hoccleve Archive v1.0, HM 111 item 01 XML at pinned commit 5a6024f
evidence.locator: commented TEI header and entire encoded item
evidence.summary: Pinned transcription identifies the Complaining Virgin item at HM 111 fols. 3r-7v, names Hoccleve, dates the holograph context 1422-1426, documents the project/editorial contributors, and declares CC BY-SA 3.0.

evidence.citation: Hoccleve Archive v1.0, HM 111 item 21 XML at pinned commit 5a6024f
evidence.locator: commented TEI header and entire encoded item
evidence.summary: Pinned transcription identifies the Regement envoy at HM 111 fols. 39v-40r, dates the holograph context 1422-1426, documents the project/editorial contributors, and declares CC BY-SA 3.0.

evidence.citation: Jennifer E. Bryan, Hoccleve, the Virgin, and the Politics of Complaint, PMLA 117.5 (2002): 1172-1187
evidence.locator: article title, abstract, and discussion of Hoccleve's Complaint of the Virgin
evidence.summary: Peer-reviewed scholarship treats the Complaint of the Virgin as Hoccleve's poem and analyzes it as a Marian complaint; combined with the HM 111 holograph and pinned transcription, it supports poem-specific responsibility without generalizing Hoccleve authorship to all fourteen appendix items.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., De Regimine Principum: A Poem by Thomas Occleve (Roxburghe Club, 1860), complete Internet Archive scan
evidence.locator: title page, preface/source statement, and complete edited poem
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms Wright's 1860 Royal MS 17 D VI-based Regement edition and distinguishes it from Furnivall's later Harley-based edition.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for deregimineprinc00jacogoog
evidence.locator: identifier, source, Google Books identifier, and rights/access fields
evidence.summary: Cached metadata identifies the Internet Archive item, Google source identifier vzIJAAAAQAAJ, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT access label.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive machine OCR for Wright's 1860 edition
evidence.locator: complete OCR file
evidence.summary: Complete target-bearing machine OCR is cached as a discovery aid; the page scan remains authoritative for readings.

evidence.citation: Charles R. Blyth, ed., Thomas Hoccleve: The Regiment of Princes, METS introduction
evidence.locator: Introduction, especially Reputation, Author and Date, Structure and Sources, and Manuscripts and Text
evidence.summary: Current scholarly introduction supports Hoccleve's authorship, composition in 1410-1411, 43 surviving manuscripts, poem structure, section boundaries, source analysis, and manuscript/editorial context. It explicitly quotes and discusses Furnivall and calls the inadequacy and relative inaccessibility of his edition reasons for a new edition.

evidence.citation: Charles R. Blyth, ed., The Regiment of Princes, METS edited text
evidence.locator: complete edited poem and line numbering
evidence.summary: Modern edited text corroborates the 5,463-line extent, old-man dialogue terminology, fifteen advice sections, and three-stanza envoy while remaining a later comparison rather than a source of Furnivall or U-M.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright and Citation
evidence.locator: copyright, citation, and permission guidance
evidence.summary: METS states the terms governing modern edition text, introductions, notes, and educational use; no open license is claimed for Blyth's modern editorial contribution.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse v1.0, fourteen records mapped to Furnivall's appendix
evidence.summary: Fourteen item records map one-to-one to the appendix. Ten records currently assign Hoccleve, while four Guillaume-linked records express French source authorship and are not converted into authorship or translation of the Middle English realizations.
notes: Item 7's Hoccleve responsibility has independent HM 111 holograph and scholarly support; the other nine Hoccleve assignments remain qualified item-level catalog attributions.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, v1.0 data release
evidence.locator: release metadata and complete tagged source archive
evidence.summary: Pinned release metadata identifies DIMEV v1.0 and its CC BY 4.0 license; the complete release archive is cached and passed ZIP integrity checking.

evidence.citation: ARLIMA, Thomas Hoccleve, The Regement of Princes
evidence.locator: work identification, manuscripts, editions, translations, and bibliography
evidence.summary: Current authority/bibliography page corroborates the Regement identity, dating, witnesses, editions including Furnivall and Blyth, and the 1997 reprint of volume III.

evidence.citation: Google Books, Hoccleve's Works II, ed. Israel Gollancz (EETS ES 73, 1925), gzuMo0nTXjkC
evidence.locator: bibliographic metadata and complete 72-image sequence
evidence.summary: The provider object identifies the 1925 volume II and supports its role in the broad three-volume U-M header; inspection confirms it contains neither the appendix selection nor the Regement target.

evidence.citation: Integrity and absence audit of complete Google Books volume-II image bundle
evidence.locator: 72 delivered page images plus exact source inventory and metadata
evidence.summary: ZIP integrity, complete provider sequence, inventory hashes, title page, table of contents, and all carrier boundaries were checked; the adq4048 target is absent.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, Hoccleve's Works: The Minor Poems, revised edition (Oxford University Press, 1970), bwb_KU-329-583
evidence.locator: item metadata and access-restricted status
evidence.summary: Metadata identifies the 1970 revised combination of EETS ES 61 and 73, with Furnivall, Gollancz, and A. I. Doyle responsibilities and access restriction. It is later edition history, not the source of adq4048.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright (April 2026)
evidence.locator: published works chart and pre-1931 public-domain statement
evidence.summary: Official current guidance states that all works published in the United States before January 1, 1931 are in the public domain; this supports the U.S.-specific status of the 1860, 1892, 1897, and 1925 editions.

open_questions.question: Which of the thirteen English Pilgrimage lyrics besides the Complaint of the Virgin can be assigned to Hoccleve, and what item-specific textual or manuscript evidence supports each assignment?
next_steps: Follow the bibliography and manuscript links in each of the nine DIMEV records currently assigning Hoccleve.
Retain each result as an item-level attribution unless independent evidence supports a stronger status.
Do not use the anonymous carrier translator or Guillaume's French-source authorship as a substitute for English lyric responsibility.

open_questions.question: What exact source and responsibility relationships underlie DIMEV records 2860, 443, 407, and 878, especially the separately transmitted Pietous complainte of the Soule?
next_steps: Inspect the four records' cited scholarship and witnesses individually.
Preserve Guillaume as author of the relevant French source passages unless a source explicitly identifies an English author or translator.
Keep appendix item 14 separate from the thirteen Pilgrimage-embedded lyrics and preserve Furnivall's reordered printed sequence.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy and production workflow generated U-M's 292 historical page-image filenames, and do those images represent every leaf of the scanned 1897 carrier?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for item-specific production metadata and the status of the historical TIFF masters.
Request a stable complete image inventory or manifest if one exists.
Do not infer complete physical-carrier coverage from the PB count or from the narrower encoded intellectual text.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's current public-domain/NoC-US statements be reconciled with the where-applicable copyright and distribution-restriction language embedded in the 2006 XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M to clarify which terms currently govern reuse of the XML, OCR, and page images.
Continue to record NoC-US as a territorial rights statement rather than a license.
Keep the legacy provider terms visible until the provider supplies a superseding item-specific statement.

open_questions.question: Will complete public images for Egerton MS 615, Harley MS 4866, and Royal MS 17 D VI return, or can complete research reproductions currently be ordered?
next_steps: Recheck each catalog record after British Library digital-service restoration milestones.
Request one item-specific quotation covering required folios, delivery resolution, reuse terms, and any condition restrictions before budgeting.
Treat the cached generic rate card as supporting service evidence only.

open_questions.question: How should each of the first 56 Regement lines in Furnivall's printed text be apportioned between the early-modern Harley replacement and Royal-derived correction or supplementation?
next_steps: Compare Furnivall's opening apparatus line by line against current images or an authorized reproduction of Harley's replacement and Royal's opening.
Keep the later replacement, Royal-supplied medieval text, and ordinary Royal collation as separate layers.
